conditions.analyte_polymer: PCL diols with uniform central block (diethylene glycol)
conditions.critical_condition_basis: Critical adsorption point (CAP) for EO block
conditions.detector: Density detector DDS70 and Refractive Index detector
conditions.evidence_text: Fig. 1 shows, that on a Synergi Fusion RP column a CAP for EO exists at 25 C in a methanol–water mobile phase containing 82 vol.% methanol. Under such conditions, two series of peaks are observed for the different PCL diols (Fig. 2)... LCCC of PCL-diols with a different middle block on Synergi Fusion RP in 82% methanol–water (CAP for EO).
conditions.notes: Analytical LCCC used to separate diblocks from triblocks based on symmetry.
conditions.physical_address: H.E.J. Research Institute of Chemistry, International Centre for Chemical and Biological Sciences (ICCBS), University of Karachi, Karachi 75270, Pakistan

conditions.critical_condition_basis: Critical adsorption point (CAP) for EO block
conditions.detector: Density detection system DDS70 and SICON LCD 201 RI detector
conditions.evidence_text: Semi-preparative LCCC of PCL-diol 830 on the Spherisorb ODS2 column in 69.1% acetonitrile. Fraction limits are indicated by dashed lines.
conditions.notes: Semi-preparative LCCC used for fractionation to identify peaks in analytical LCCC. Included as it is an LCCC experiment, though preparative in scale.
conditions.physical_address: H.E.J. Research Institute of Chemistry, International Centre for Chemical and Biological Sciences (ICCBS), University of Karachi, Karachi 75270, Pakistan

conditions.critical_condition_basis: Critical adsorption point (CAP) for PEG/EO block
conditions.detector: Density detector DDS70 and Refractive Index detector
conditions.evidence_text: In principle, with monodisperse central block or a short central block with low polydispersity, the separation according to BO is possible around critical adsorption point of PEG... Fig. 10 shows overlays of chromatograms... with lower methanol content (70%), lower oligomers can be separated according to symmetry (Fig. 10a).
conditions.notes: Text states separation is possible 'around critical adsorption point' and uses 70% methanol for symmetry separation. The exact CAP for BO-DEG-BO is not explicitly defined as a single number like the PCL case, but the condition is used for LCCC-like separation.
conditions.physical_address: H.E.J. Research Institute of Chemistry, International Centre for Chemical and Biological Sciences (ICCBS), University of Karachi, Karachi 75270, Pakistan
